response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned full-stack/web engineer with decades of experience, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. The position requires native-level Japanese proficiency, strong C for UNIX development, Linux kernel source-code analysis and modification, and hands-on experience with embedded LAN-switch security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Layer-2/3 networking). The candidate lists only English, Romanian, and German; C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments; and there is no evidence of kernel work, network-switch development, or security-protocol implementation. The hard language requirement alone is disqualifying.
strengths: Long, diverse software career spanning web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded exposure
Some C/C++ experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) and Linux familiarity
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a hard requirement (native level)
No Linux kernel, LAN-switch, or embedded networking security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF)
Primary stack (TypeScript/Node.js/D) is far from C-for-UNIX embedded firmware development

key_factors.factor: General engineering depth and Linux familiarity
